Phrase Free Translation: I want to give you the history of my parent

Transcription: Tite mi su fu Gidam.
Phrase Free Translation: My father is from Gidam

Transcription: Nga kpo yinga weiyini, mo reshekə ni Gidam re, ryem kyang bo yhag, mon tu'u nzang.
Phrase Free Translation: He married one wife, as they were staying together the issue of farmland came up with his brothers (They were protecting the land from the bush fire)

Transcription: Tite mi su whei zhini ni ngebe rimi zangma
Phrase Free Translation: My father was one of those that quenched the fire that was burning

Transcription: ferɔ nuk ma kem, nga moh hera nga tu'u nzang ma.
Phrase Free Translation: that preserved the place

Transcription: Moh dzi gabsa shema, nei ngwo tite mi su sə kponga shiyinga tsu ka bohsi nga.
Phrase Free Translation: when they were about to share the portion, the younger brother of my father chose first. That annoyed my father.

Transcription: Sheka men tite mi shirikɔ.
Phrase Free Translation: That is when my father got annoyed.

Transcription: Nga kɘ sokkoh Gidam re, singsi nga whor Gber.
Phrase Free Translation: He left Gidam and moved down to Gber (Hausa: Aribi)

Transcription: Nga su nei yab, kɘ ku tu balm. Nga dzi ri tongda serre, whor gberma nga yha gbɘr shabɔ (ni ki).
Phrase Free Translation: He was a hunter, and a palm wine tapper. As he moved down he saw the land was good. He started clearing the farm

Transcription: Nga gber shabɔ, feri kyanga, juoro kɘ bɔ nga bo khɘk yare, nikur weiya.
Phrase Free Translation: When it was rainy season, he came and picked our mum, with the rest of the children.

Transcription: Kɘ dziniki, mo dzi sherniki, kɘ dɘng riamɔ, kɘ dɘng tu balmɔ, kɘ dɘng yab.
Phrase Free Translation: He went there and they settled there and they continued with the farming activities, the palm wine tapping, and the hunting.

Transcription: Ridzokɘ tite mi sokɘ kpeng ko eke. Mo mar ami ma niki ni whor Gber ma.
Phrase Free Translation: This is what my father did. I personally was born there at the Gber side.

Transcription: Mo kɘ dzimɔ wur yabmanɔ mokɘ tu weisu weinɔ, nga be na kek mɔ duo.
Phrase Free Translation: When they are about to go for hunting, they used to assign little children who have not been introduced to the world,

Transcription: Mo dzimɔ yha per sekɘ.
Phrase Free Translation: they are the people who are to enter the forest to chase out the animals

Transcription: Mo tite ngebe bang, tamɔ ni rigbi dzi wang siabkɘ nyamɔ bowur mo gbor.
Phrase Free Translation: The parent with bows and arrows will follow the sides of the forest when the animals come out, they will shoot.

Transcription: Sherma moshekɘ niki, mowok jeyi niki, ni nyamɔ ba nyame maferemɔ.
Phrase Free Translation: In staying there, they got enough food and also killed a lot of animals there. No kind of animal that they did not kill there.

Transcription: Ni rigbi manɔ be nyi yang, ngekɘ fu nasarɔ redɘng banga nyebe, rigbi mo tite re resok kɘriyo.
Phrase Free Translation: It was with arrows, not modern weapons of the white man that they used. It is our forefather's arrows that we grew up and saw them with.

Transcription: Resher nishe manɔ wormiakɘ sokatah kɘ gar.
Phrase Free Translation: We stayed in that place for thirty years and above.

Transcription: Kitɔ mɔ tite mi bori shekɘ bosanɔ, are weiya ma reri shekɘ agoyo ba ghi.
Phrase Free Translation: Before my parents observed  that the situation had changed. We the children also observed that, because there was no road leading to that place.

Transcription: Ghikɘ dziko, khenakɘ dzingu niki ko rong, ko ngu dɘng tattɘ ki, modah kpanimɔ kpongu wur dzondzon.
Phrase Free Translation: The road that would convey somebody down there even if you are at the point of death they won't find the means of conveying you out on time.

Transcription: Niki re hanghanga bemo tite re re to re gur dzire be ware.
Phrase Free Translation: That is where we spoke to our parents, that we should go back to our brothers.

Transcription: Tite re go hanga gwonko, nga kə rem benei yhu nga yare.
Phrase Free Translation: When our father heard what we said, he then spoke to our mother.

Transcription: Weibe rahangna reinkpein hɔ?
Phrase Free Translation: These children are talking - how do we do?

Transcription: Yare gei denga ngu go weiya sisobowur kə rehangna regur dzire hakə.
Phrase Free Translation: Our mother then said since our children have started talking about it, let's go back home.

Transcription: Niki resok, redzire sok nishemanɔ, re khek jeyiye, netong rikə gohyong.
Phrase Free Translation: That is where we live, when we were living in that place we left with plenty of food which people when they saw it they were afraid. We brought it home.

Transcription: Rekpobo hakə, yosu, shukə tuk re sokre nishema, ryem da ghi, boreki woni su ni shema/ki har nyebe.
Phrase Free Translation: That is the reason we left that place. Issue of road. If not we could have still been there up to now.

Transcription: Jiye mi sufu remɔ ngeye.
Phrase Free Translation: That is all I have to say
